This is used only for reading or\\n46:  * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\\n47:  * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\\n48:  */\\n49: public class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\\n50:     public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\\n51: \\n52:     private final ByteBuffer buffer;\\n53: \\n54:     private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\\n55: \\n56:     private int validBytes = -1;\\n57: \\n58:     // Construct a writable memory records\\n59:     private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\\n60:         Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \\\"buffer should not be null\\\");\\n61:         this.buffer = buffer;\\n62:     }\\n63: \\n64:     @Override\\n65:     public int sizeInBytes() {\\n66:         return buffer.limit();\\n67:     }\\n68: \\n69:     @Override\\n70:     public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\\n71:         if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\\n72:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \\\"\\n73:                     + position + \\\", length: \\\" + length + \\\", buffer.limit(): \\\" + buffer.limit());\\n74: \\n75:         return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\\n76:     }\\n77: \\n78:     /**\\n79:      * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\\n80:      * @param channel The channel to write to\\n81:      * @return The number of bytes written\\n82:      * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\\n83:      */\\n84:     public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\\n85:         buffer.mark();\\n86:         int written = 0;\\n87:         while (written < sizeInBytes())\\n88:             written += channel.write(buffer);\\n89:         buffer.reset();\\n90:         return written;\\n91:     }\\n92: \\n93:     /**\\n94:      * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\\n95:      * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\\n96:      * @return The number of valid bytes\\n97:      */\\n98:     public int validBytes() {\\n99:         if (validBytes >= 0)\\n100:             return validBytes;\\n101: \\n102:         int bytes = 0;\\n103:         for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\\n104:             bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\\n105: \\n106:         this.validBytes = bytes;\\n107:         return bytes;\\n108:     }\\n109: \\n110:     @Override\\n111:     public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\\n112:         return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\\n113:     }\\n114: \\n115:     /**\\n116:      * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\\n117:      * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\\n118:      *         magic byte, null otherwise\\n119:      * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\\n120:      */\\n121:     public Integer firstBatchSize() {\\n122:         if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\\n123:             return null;\\n124:         return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\\n125:     }\\n126: \\n127:     /**\\n128:      * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\\n129:      *\\n130:      * @param filter                      The filter function\\n131:      * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\\n132:      * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\\n133:      *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\\n134:      *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\\n135:      *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\\n136:      *                                    performance impact.\\n137:      * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\\n138:      */\\n139:     public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\\n140:         return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\\n141:     }\\n142: \\n143:     /**\\n144:      * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\\n145:      * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\\n146:      */\\n147:     private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\\n148:                                          ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\\n149:         FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\\n150:         ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\\n151:         for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\\n152:             final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\\n153:             final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\\n154:             final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\\n155: \\n156:             filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\\n157: \\n158:             if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\\n159:                 continue;\\n160: \\n161:             final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\\n162:                 filter);\\n163:             List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\\n164:             boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\\n165:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\\n166:             long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\\n167: \\n168:             if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\\n169:                 // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\\n170:                 // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\\n171:                 // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\\n172:                 boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\\n173:                     batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\\n174:                 if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\\n175:                     batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\\n176:                     filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\\n177:                 } else {\\n178:                     long deleteHorizonMs;\\n179:                     if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\\n180:                         deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\\n181:                     else\\n182:                         deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\\n183:                     try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\\n184:                             bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\\n185:                         MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\\n186:                         int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\\n187:                         MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\\n188:                         filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\\n189:                             maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\\n190:                     }\\n191:                 }\\n192:             } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\\n193:                 if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\\n194:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\\\");\\n195: \\n196:                 bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\\n197:                 DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\\n198:                         batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\\n199:                         batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\\n200:                         batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\\n201:                 filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\\n202:             }\\n203: \\n204:             // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\\n205:             // avoid the need for additional allocations.\\n206:             ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\\n207:             if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\\n208:                 filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\\n209:                 return filterResult;\\n210:             }\\n211:         }\\n212: \\n213:         return filterResult;\\n214:     }\\n215: \\n216:     private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\\n217:                                                  BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\\n218:                                                  FilterResult filterResult,\\n219:                                                  RecordFilter filter) {\\n220:         try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\\n221:             long maxOffset = -1;\\n222:             boolean containsTombstones = false;\\n223:             // Convert records with old record versions\\n224:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\\n225:             List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\\n226:             while (iterator.hasNext()) {\\n227:                 Record record = iterator.next();\\n228:                 filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\\n229: \\n230:                 if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\\n231:                     if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\\n232:                         maxOffset = record.offset();\\n233: \\n234:                     retainedRecords.add(record);\\n235: \\n236:                     if (!record.hasValue()) {\\n237:                         containsTombstones = true;\\n238:                     }\\n239:                 } else {\\n240:                     writeOriginalBatch = false;\\n241:                 }\\n242:             }\\n243:             return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\\n244:         }\\n245:     }\\n246: \\n247:     private static class BatchFilterResult {\\n248:         private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\\n249:         private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\\n250:         private final boolean containsTombstones;\\n251:         private final long maxOffset;\\n252:         private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\\n253:                                   final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\\n254:                                   final boolean containsTombstones,\\n255:                                   final long maxOffset) {\\n256:             this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\\n257:             this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\\n258:             this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\\n259:             this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\\n260:         }\\n261:     }\\n262: \\n263:     private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\\n264:                                                                  List<Record> retainedRecords,\\n265:                                                                  ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\\n266:                                                                  final long deleteHorizonMs) {\\n267:         Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\\n268:         // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\\n269:         // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\\n270:         // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\\n271:         // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\\n272:         TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\\n273:                 TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\\n274:         long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\\n275:                 originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\\n276:         long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\\n277:                 originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\\n278: \\n279:         // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\\n280:         MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\\n281:                 compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\\n282:                 originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\\n283:                 originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\\n284: \\n285:         for (Record record : retainedRecords)\\n286:             builder.append(record);\\n287: \\n288:         if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\\n289:             // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\\n290:             // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\\n291:             // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\\n292:             builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\\n293: \\n294:         return builder;\\n295:     }\\n296: \\n297:     /**\\n298:      * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\\n299:      */\\n300:     public ByteBuffer buffer() {\\n301:         return buffer.duplicate();\\n302:     }\\n303: \\n304:     @Override\\n305:     public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\\n306:         if (position < 0)\\n307:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Invalid position: \\\" + position + \\\" in read from \\\" + this);\\n308:         if (position > buffer.limit())\\n309:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Slice from position \\\" + position + \\\" exceeds end position of \\\" + this);\\n310:         if (size < 0)\\n311:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Invalid size: \\\" + size + \\\" in read from \\\" + this);\\n312: \\n313:         int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\\n314:         // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\\n315:         // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\\n316:         // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\\n317:         // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\\n318:         // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\\n319:         // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\\n320:         ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\\n321:         slicedBuffer.position(position);\\n322:         slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\\n323:         // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\\n324:         slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\\n325: \\n326:         return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\\n327:     }\\n328: \\n329:     @Override\\n330:     public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\\n331:         return batches;\\n332:     }\\n333: \\n334:     @Override\\n335:     public String toString() {\\n336:         return \\\"MemoryRecords(size=\\\" + sizeInBytes() +\\n337:                 \\\", buffer=\\\" + buffer +\\n338:                 \\\")\\\";\\n339:     }\\n340: \\n341:     @Override\\n342:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\\n343:         if (this == o)\\n344:             return true;\\n345:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\\n346:             return false;\\n347: \\n348:         MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\\n349: \\n350:         return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\\n351:     }\\n352: \\n353:     @Override\\n354:     public int hashCode() {\\n355:         return buffer.hashCode();\\n356:     }\\n357: \\n358:     public abstract static class RecordFilter {\\n359:         public final long currentTime;\\n360:         public final long deleteRetentionMs;\\n361: \\n362:         public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\\n363:             this.currentTime = currentTime;\\n364:             this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\\n365:         }\\n366: \\n367:         public static class BatchRetentionResult {\\n368:             public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\\n369:             public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\\n370:             public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\\n371:                                         final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\\n372:                 this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\\n373:                 this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\\n374:             }\\n375:         }\\n376: \\n377:         public enum BatchRetention {\\n378:             DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\\n379:             RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\\n380:             DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\\n381:         }\\n382: \\n383:         /**\\n384:          * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\\n385:          * check the records individually).\\n386:          */\\n387:         protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\\n388: \\n389:         /**\\n390:          * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\\n391:          * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\\n392:          * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\\n393:          */\\n394:         protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\\n395:     }\\n396: \\n397:     public static class FilterResult {\\n398:         private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\\n399:         private int messagesRead = 0;\\n400:         // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\\n401:         // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\\n402:         private int bytesRead = 0;\\n403:         private int messagesRetained = 0;\\n404:         private int bytesRetained = 0;\\n405:         private long maxOffset = -1L;\\n406:         private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\\n407:         private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\\n408: \\n409:         private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\\n410:             this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\\n411:         }\\n412: \\n413:         private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\\n414:             int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\\n415:             updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\\n416:                     retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\\n417:         }\\n418: \\n419:         private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\\n422:             if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\\n423:                 this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\\n424:                 this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\\n425:             }\\n426:             this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\\n427:             this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\\n428:             this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\\n429:         }\\n430: \\n431:         private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\\n432:             if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\\n433:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \\\" + maxTimestamp);\\n434:             if (maxOffset < 0)\\n435:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"maxOffset undefined\\\");\\n436:         }\\n437: \\n438:         public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\\n439:             return outputBuffer;\\n440:         }\\n441: \\n442:         public int messagesRead() {\\n443:             return messagesRead;\\n444:         }\\n445: \\n446:         public int bytesRead() {\\n447:             return bytesRead;\\n448:         }\\n449: \\n450:         public int messagesRetained() {\\n451:             return messagesRetained;\\n452:         }\\n453: \\n454:         public int bytesRetained() {\\n455:             return bytesRetained;\\n456:         }\\n457: \\n458:         public long maxOffset() {\\n459:             return    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,\\n706:                                                    int partitionLeaderEpoch, long MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n60:         Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n61:         this.buffer = buffer;\n62:     }\n63: \n64:     @Override\n65:     public int sizeInBytes() {\n66:         return buffer.limit();\n67:     }\n68: \n69:     @Override\n70:     public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n71:         if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n72:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n73:                     + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n74: \n75:         return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n76:     }\n77: \n78:     /**\n79:      * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n80:      * @param channel The channel to write to\n81:      * @return The number of bytes written\n82:      * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n83:      */\n84:     public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n85:         buffer.mark();\n86:         int written = 0;\n87:         while (written < sizeInBytes())\n88:             written += channel.write(buffer);\n89:         buffer.reset();\n90:         return written;\n91:     }\n92: \n93:     /**\n94:      * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\n95:      * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n96:      * @return The number of valid bytes\n97:      */\n98:     public int validBytes() {\n99:         if (validBytes >= 0)\n100:             return validBytes;\n101: \n102:         int bytes = 0;\n103:         for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n104:             bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n105: \n106:         this.validBytes = bytes;\n107:         return bytes;\n108:     }\n109: \n110:     @Override\n111:     public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n112:         return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n113:     }\n114: \n115:     /**\n116:      * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n117:      * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n118:      *         magic byte, null otherwise\n119:      * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n120:      */\n121:     public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n122:         if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n123:             return null;\n124:         return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n125:     }\n126: \n127:     /**\n128:      * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\n129:      *\n130:      * @param filter                      The filter function\n131:      * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\n132:      * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\n133:      *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\n134:      *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n135:      *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n136:      *                                    performance impact.\n137:      * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\n138:      */\n139:     public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n140:         return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n141:     }\n142: \n143:     /**\n144:      * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n145:      * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n146:      */\n147:     private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n148:                                          ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n149:         FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n150:         ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n151:         for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n152:             final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n153:             final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n154:             final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n155: \n156:             filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n157: \n158:             if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n159:                 continue;\n160: \n161:             final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n162:                 filter);\n163:             List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n164:             boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n165:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n166:             long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n167: \n168:             if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n169:                 // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n170:                 // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n171:                 // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n172:                 boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n173:                     batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n174:                 if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n175:                     batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n176:                     filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n177:                 } else {\n178:                     long deleteHorizonMs;\n179:                     if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n180:                         deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n181:                     else\n182:                         deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n183:                     try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n184:                             bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n185:                         MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n186:                         int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n187:                         MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n188:                         filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n189:                             maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n190:                     }\n191:                 }\n192:             } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n193:                 if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n194:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n195: \n196:                 bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n197:                 DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n198:                         batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n199:                         batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n200:                         batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n201:                 filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n202:             }\n203: \n204:             // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n205:             // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n206:             ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n207:             if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n208:                 filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n209:                 return filterResult;\n210:             }\n211:         }\n212: \n213:         return filterResult;\n214:     }\n215: \n216:     private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n217:                                                  BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n218:                                                  FilterResult filterResult,\n219:                                                  RecordFilter filter) {\n220:         try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n221:             long maxOffset = -1;\n222:             boolean containsTombstones = false;\n223:             // Convert records with old record versions\n224:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n225:             List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n226:             while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n227:                 Record record = iterator.next();\n228:                 filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n229: \n230:                 if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n231:                     if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n232:                         maxOffset = record.offset();\n233: \n234:                     retainedRecords.add(record);\n235: \n236:                     if (!record.hasValue()) {\n237:                         containsTombstones = true;\n238:                     }\n239:                 } else {\n240:                     writeOriginalBatch = false;\n241:                 }\n242:             }\n243:             return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n244:         }\n245:     }\n246: \n247:     private static class BatchFilterResult {\n248:         private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n249:         private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n250:         private final boolean containsTombstones;\n251:         private final long maxOffset;\n252:         private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n253:                                   final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n254:                                   final boolean containsTombstones,\n255:                                   final long maxOffset) {\n256:             this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n257:             this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n258:             this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n259:             this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n260:         }\n261:     }\n262: \n263:     private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n264:                                                                  List<Record> retainedRecords,\n265:                                                                  ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n266:                                                                  final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n267:         Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n268:         // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n269:         // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n270:         // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n271:         // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n272:         TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n273:                 TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n274:         long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n275:                 originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n276:         long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n277:                 originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n278: \n279:         // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n280:         MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n281:                 compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n282:                 originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n283:                 originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n284: \n285:         for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n286:             builder.append(record);\n287: \n288:         if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n289:             // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n290:             // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n291:             // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n292:             builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n293: \n294:         return builder;\n295:     }\n296: \n297:     /**\n298:      * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n299:      */\n300:     public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n301:         return buffer.duplicate();\n302:     }\n303: \n304:     @Override\n305:     public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n306:         if (position < 0)\n307:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n308:         if (position > buffer.limit())\n309:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n310:         if (size < 0)\n311:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n312: \n313:         int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n314:         // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n315:         // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n316:         // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n317:         // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n318:         // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n319:         // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n320:         ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n321:         slicedBuffer.position(position);\n322:         slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n323:         // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n324:         slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n325: \n326:         return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n327:     }\n328: \n329:     @Override\n330:     public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n331:         return batches;\n332:     }\n333: \n334:     @Override\n335:     public String toString() {\n336:         return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n337:                 \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n338:                 \")\";\n339:     }\n340: \n341:     @Override\n342:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\n343:         if (this == o)\n344:             return true;\n345:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n346:             return false;\n347: \n348:         MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n349: \n350:         return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n351:     }\n352: \n353:     @Override\n354:     public int hashCode() {\n355:         return buffer.hashCode();\n356:     }\n357: \n358:     public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n359:         public final long currentTime;\n360:         public final     }\n376: \n377:         public enum BatchRetention {\n378:             DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n379:             RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n380:             DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n381:         }\n382: \n383:         /**\n384:          * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even Only records from batches which were not\n392:          * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n393:          */\n394:         protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n395:     }\n396: \n397:     public static class FilterResult {\n398:         private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n399:         private int messagesRead = 0;\n400:         // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n401:         // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n402:         private int bytesRead = 0;\n403:         private int messagesRetained = 0;\n404:         private int bytesRetained = 0;\n405:         private long maxOffset = -1L;\n406:         private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n407:         private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n408: \n409:         private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n410:             this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n411:         }\n412: \n413:         private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n414:             int bytesRetained = headerOnly ?  retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n417:         }\n418: \n419:         private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset);\n422:             if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n423:                 this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n424:                 this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n425:             }\n426:             this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n427:             this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n428:             this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n429:         }\n430: \n431:         private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n432:             if }\n461: \n462:         public long maxTimestamp() {\n463:             return maxTimestamp;\n464:         }\n465: \n466:         public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n467:             return                  int maxSize) {\n487:         long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n488:         if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n489:             logAppendTime = {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01U9ZGc4jHLU3TfGhBXsVh3y","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017GheaYdkWwYkef2KZfGnu1","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/RecordBatch.java","content":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread-safety:</strong> RecordBatch implementations are <strong>not thread-safe</strong>.\n * Batches backed by ByteBuffer are particularly vulnerable to concurrent modification. Multiple threads\n * should not access the same batch concurrently. However, different batches can be safely accessed by\n * different threads in parallel.\n *\n * <p><strong>Usage:</strong> Record batches are typically accessed through {@link MemoryRecords} or\n * {@link Records} implementations, which manage the underlying buffer lifecycle. Direct instantiation\n * of batch implementations is uncommon in normal usage.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true if the checksum is valid, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     *\n     * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException if the checksum is invalid\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     * Uses CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time ({@link TimestampType#CREATE_TIME}), this is the max\n     * timestamp among all records contained in this batch and the value is updated during log compaction.\n     * If the timestamp type is log append time, this value is set by the broker.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp in milliseconds\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type, never null\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> For older magic versions (0 and 1), this method may require\n     * iterating through records to determine the base offset. Use {@link #lastOffset()} instead if efficiency\n     * is critical, as it is always efficient across all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> This method is efficient for all magic versions and does not\n     * require record iteration.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte (0, 1, or 2)\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return\n     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID} (-1).\n     *\n     * <p>The producer id is used for idempotence and transactional semantics. Only magic version 2+\n     * batches can have meaningful producer IDs.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID} if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return\n     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH} (-1).\n     *\n     * <p>The producer epoch is paired with the producer ID to support idempotence. Epochs are incremented\n     * when a producer is fenced by a new generation.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or {@link #NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH} if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set. A batch has a valid producer id if the id is greater than\n     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID}.\n     *\n     * @return true if this batch has a valid producer id, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * <p>The sequence number is used for idempotent and transactional producers to detect duplicates and\n     * out-of-order messages.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or {@link #NO_SEQUENCE} if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or {@link #NO_SEQUENCE} if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type ({@link CompressionType#NONE}, {@link CompressionType#GZIP},\n     *         {@link CompressionType#SNAPPY}, {@link CompressionType#LZ4}, or {@link CompressionType#ZSTD})\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of all records and the batch overhead (header).\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> This is an O(1) operation.\n     *\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count of records in this batch if it is efficiently supported by the record format.\n     * This is only efficiently supported for magic version 2 and higher.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> For magic 0 and 1, returns null to indicate that record\n     * count is not readily available without iteration.\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch, or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     *\n     * @return true if the batch uses any compression codec, false if uncompressed ({@link CompressionType#NONE})\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer. The batch is serialized in its entirety, including headers\n     * and all records.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to. The buffer must have sufficient remaining space\n     *               for the entire batch ({@link #sizeInBytes()})\n     * @throws java.nio.BufferOverflowException if the buffer does not have sufficient remaining space\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     *\n     * <p>Transactional batches contain records from a single atomic operation and will either all be committed\n     * or all be aborted. Transactional batches are marked with the transactional flag in the batch attributes.\n     *\n     * @return true if this batch is part of a transaction, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon timestamp, which indicates the time at which tombstone records and aborted\n     * transaction markers in this batch should be removed during log compaction.\n     *\n     * <p>This value is only set for batches where tombstones or aborted transactional markers are being\n     * cleaned up. It represents a timestamp in milliseconds.\n     *\n     * @return {@link OptionalLong#empty()} if the delete horizon is not set for this batch, otherwise\n     *         an OptionalLong containing the delete horizon timestamp\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch. This epoch is assigned by the broker when\n     * the batch is first written and does not change with leadership transitions.\n     *\n     * @return The leader epoch (>= 0) or {@link #NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH} (-1) if unknown (typically\n     *         for batches produced before the leader epoch was assigned)\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which delays decompression of the record stream until the records are\n     * actually requested using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the record format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, the normal iterator is returned instead.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance characteristics:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>For uncompressed batches: Returns a minimal iterator with no decompression overhead\n     *   <li>For compressed batches: Decompresses data on-demand as records are consumed, which can\n     *       significantly reduce memory usage for large batches\n     *   <li>Decompression buffers are obtained from the supplier, allowing buffer reuse for performance\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Resource management:</strong> The returned iterator must be properly closed using\n     * try-with-resources or explicit {@link CloseableIterator#close()} to release decompression resources.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression. For small\n     *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating. A supplier that\n     *                                    reuses buffers will have significant performance impact.\n     * @return A closeable iterator over records in this batch. The iterator must be closed.\n     * @see BufferSupplier#create() for creating a new non-reusing supplier\n     * @see BufferSupplier#NO_CACHING for a caching-disabled supplier\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * Control batches contain metadata records such as end-of-transaction markers, leadership changes, etc.\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this always returns false.\n     *\n     * @return true if this batch contains control records, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * Iterate all records in this batch to find the offset of the maximum timestamp.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Notes:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>If multiple records have the same maximum timestamp, the earliest offset is returned\n     *   <li>Always returns {@link Optional#empty()} if the batch magic equals {@link #MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     *       (since V0 does not support timestamps)\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> This method requires iterating through all records in the batch\n     * and decompressing if necessary. Use with caution on large batches.\n     *\n     * @return {@link Optional#empty()} if no max timestamp offset exists (V0 batches), otherwise an\n     *         Optional containing the offset of the record with maximum timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":531,"cache_read_input_tokens":75599,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":531},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2eb2b5a7-b97c-4215-816d-bb7ea970b64f","uuid":"9103e92a-5a2a-45d1-b463-294248df8ae4"}

In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread-safety:</strong> RecordBatch implementations are <strong>not thread-safe</strong>.\n * Batches backed by ByteBuffer are particularly vulnerable to concurrent modification. Multiple threads\n * should not access the same batch concurrently. However, different batches can be safely accessed by\n * different threads in parallel.\n *\n * <p><strong>Usage:</strong> Record batches are typically accessed through {@link MemoryRecords} or\n * {@link Records} implementations, which manage the underlying buffer lifecycle. Direct instantiation\n * of batch implementations is uncommon in normal usage.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true if the checksum is valid, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     *\n     * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException if the checksum is invalid\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     * Uses CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time ({@link TimestampType#CREATE_TIME}), this is the max\n     * timestamp among all records contained in this batch and the value is updated during log compaction.\n     * If the timestamp type is log append time, this value is set by the broker.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp in milliseconds\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type, never null\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> For older magic versions (0 and 1), this method may require\n     * iterating through records to determine the base offset. Use {@link #lastOffset()} instead if efficiency\n     * is critical, as it is always efficient across all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> This method is efficient for all magic versions and does not\n     * require record iteration.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte (0, 1, or 2)\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return\n     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID} (-1).\n     *\n     * <p>The producer id is used for idempotence and transactional semantics. Only magic version 2+\n     * batches can have meaningful producer IDs.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID} if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return\n     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH} (-1).\n     *\n     * <p>The producer epoch is paired with the producer ID to support idempotence. Epochs are incremented\n     * when a producer is fenced by a new generation.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or {@link #NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH} if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set. A batch has a valid producer id if the id is greater than\n     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID}.\n     *\n     * @return true if this batch has a valid producer id, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or {@link #NO_SEQUENCE} if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type ({@link CompressionType#NONE}, {@link CompressionType#GZIP},\n     *         {@link CompressionType#SNAPPY}, {@link CompressionType#LZ4}, or {@link CompressionType#ZSTD})\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of all records and the batch overhead (header).\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> This is an O(1) operation.\n     *\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count of records in this batch if it is efficiently supported by the record format.\n     * This is only efficiently supported for magic version 2 and higher.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> For magic 0 and 1, returns null to indicate that record\n     * count is not readily available without iteration.\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch, or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     *\n     * @return true if the batch uses any compression codec, false if uncompressed ({@link CompressionType#NONE})\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer. The batch is serialized in its entirety, including headers\n     * and all records.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to. The buffer must have sufficient remaining space\n     *               for the entire batch ({@link #sizeInBytes()})\n     * @throws java.nio.BufferOverflowException if the buffer does not have sufficient remaining space\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     *\n     * <p>Transactional batches contain records from a single atomic operation and will either all be committed\n     * or all be aborted. Transactional batches are marked with the transactional flag in the batch attributes.\n     *\n     * @return true if this batch is part of a transaction, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon timestamp, which indicates the time at which tombstone records and aborted\n     * transaction markers in this batch should be removed during log compaction.\n     *\n     * <p>This value is only set for batches where tombstones or aborted transactional markers are being\n     * cleaned up. This epoch is assigned by the broker when\n     * the batch is first written and does not change with leadership transitions.\n     *\n     * @return The leader epoch (>= 0) or {@link #NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH} (-1) if unknown (typically\n     *         for batches produced before the leader epoch was assigned)\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which delays decompression of the record stream until the records are\n     * actually requested using {@link Iterator#next()}. The iterator must be closed.\n     * @see BufferSupplier#create() for creating a new non-reusing supplier\n     * @see BufferSupplier#NO_CACHING for a caching-disabled supplier\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * Control batches contain metadata records such as end-of-transaction markers, leadership changes, etc.\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this always returns false.\n     *\n     * @return true if this batch contains control records, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * Iterate all records in this batch to find the offset of the maximum timestamp.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Notes:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>If multiple records have the same maximum timestamp, the earliest offset is returned\n     *   <li>Always returns {@link Optional#empty()} if the batch magic equals {@link #MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     *       (since V0 does not support timestamps)\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> This method requires iterating through all records in the batch\n     * and decompressing if necessary. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStream;\nimport java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.NoSuchElementException;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n\n/**\n * {@link RecordBatch} implementation for magic version 2 and above. This class encapsulates the\n * binary serialization format of a Kafka record batch, including the batch header and all contained\n * records.\n *\n * <p><strong>Binary Format:</strong> The batch follows the RecordBatch schema:\n * <pre>\n * RecordBatch =>\n *  BaseOffset => Int64\n *  Length => Int32\n *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n *  Magic => Int8\n *  CRC => Uint32\n *  Attributes => Int16\n *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32\n *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n *  ProducerId => Int64\n *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n *  BaseSequence => Int32\n *  RecordsCount => Int32\n *  Records => [Record]\n * </pre>\n * When compression is enabled, the compressed record data follows immediately after the record count.\n *\n * <p><strong>CRC Computation:</strong> The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used and covers all\n * bytes from the attributes field through the end of the batch. The partition leader epoch field is\n * excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation when the broker assigns a leader epoch.\n *\n * <p><strong>Log Compaction:</strong> Unlike older message formats, magic v2 batches preserve the\n * first and last offset/sequence numbers from the original batch during log compaction. This preservation\n * is critical for: (1) restoring producer state on broker restart, and (2) duplicate detection via\n * sequence number verification. Empty batches may be retained to preserve producer sequence information\n * until a new sequence is written or the producer ID expires.\n *\n * <p><strong>Timestamp Semantics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>BaseTimestamp: For CREATE_TIME, reflects the first record's timestamp. After compaction,\n *       reflects the first remaining record. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, set by the broker. If the\n *       delete horizon flag is set, BaseTimestamp contains the horizon timestamp for cleanup.</li>\n *   <li>MaxTimestamp: For CREATE_TIME, the maximum timestamp among current records and updated during\n *       compaction. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, set by the broker and preserved across compaction.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Attributes:</strong> Encoded in a 16-bit field with the following structure:\n * <pre>\n *  Bits 0-2:   Compression Type (0=None, 1=GZIP, 2=Snappy, 3=LZ4, 4=ZSTD)\n *  Bit 3:      Timestamp Type (0=CreateTime, 1=LogAppendTime)\n *  Bit 4:      Is Transactional\n *  Bit 5:      Is Control Batch\n *  Bit 6:      Delete Horizon Flag\n *  Bits 7-15:  Reserved (unused)\n * </pre>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread-safety:</strong> {@code DefaultRecordBatch} is <strong>not thread-safe</strong>.\n * The underlying ByteBuffer backing a batch is mutable and can be modified by multiple threads.\n * To ensure thread-safety: (1) do not share batch instances across threads, or (2) use external\n * synchronization if sharing is necessary, or (3) wrap the batch operations with immutable views.\n * Multiple distinct batch instances can be safely accessed in parallel from different threads.\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Header access (offset, timestamp, producer id, etc.) is O(1)</li>\n *   <li>Record iteration is O(n) but amortized per-record cost is minimal for uncompressed batches</li>\n *   <li>Decompression on iteration can be a bottleneck; use {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n *       with buffer reuse for memory efficiency on large batches</li>\n *   <li>CRC validation is O(n) in batch size and is performed lazily on demand</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch\n * @see MemoryRecords\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder\n */\npublic class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n    static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n    public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n    static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n    public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n    static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n    public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n    static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n    public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n\n    private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n    private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n    private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n    private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n    private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    /**\n     * Constructs a DefaultRecordBatch from a ByteBuffer. The buffer is expected to contain\n     * a complete batch with valid header and record data. The buffer position and limit define\n     * the boundaries of the batch data.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Note:</strong> The batch maintains a reference to the provided buffer. Callers\n     * should ensure the buffer remains valid and unmodified for the lifetime of the batch.\n     *\n     * @param buffer A ByteBuffer containing the serialized batch data. Must contain at least\n     *               {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} bytes.\n     */\n    DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte magic() {\n        return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the integrity of this record batch. Checks that the batch size is sufficient\n     * to contain at least the batch header overhead, and that the CRC checksum is correct.\n     *\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the batch is smaller than the required overhead or\n     *                                if the stored CRC does not match the computed CRC\n     */\n    @Override\n    public void ensureValid() {\n        if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \" + sizeInBytes() +\n                    \" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \")\");\n\n        if (!isValid())\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \" + checksum()\n                    + \", computed crc = \" + computeChecksum() + \")\");\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used as the reference for calculating\n     * relative record timestamps (timestamp deltas). The interpretation of this value depends\n     * on the timestamp type and whether the delete horizon flag is set.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Semantics:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>If delete horizon is set: Contains the delete horizon timestamp for cleanup</li>\n     *   <li>If CREATE_TIME: Timestamp of the first record in the batch (or first after compaction)</li>\n     *   <li>If LOG_APPEND_TIME: Set by broker when batch is appended to log</li>\n     *   <li>If batch is empty: Set to {@link RecordBatch#NO_TIMESTAMP}</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * @return The base timestamp in milliseconds\n     */\n    public long baseTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long maxTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public TimestampType timestampType() {\n        return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ?
