Batches are deserialized\n     * lazily as the iterable is traversed; no deserialization occurs until iteration begins.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance note:</strong> Iterable may be traversed multiple times, with each\n     * iteration creating fresh batch views of the underlying buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Modification safety:</strong> The returned iterable should not be used if the\n     * underlying buffer is being concurrently modified.\n     *\n     * @return An iterable of {@link MutableRecordBatch} instances. Never null.\n     */\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }","originalFile":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer for reading and in-place modification\n * of existing record batch data. This class provides memory-efficient access to serialized Kafka\n * record batches without requiring disk I/O.\n *\n * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong> MemoryRecords is used for three primary scenarios:\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading:</strong> Deserializing record batches from a ByteBuffer (e.g., from fetch responses)</li>\n *   <li><strong>In-place modification:</strong> Updating batch metadata (timestamps, offsets) without copying</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> Removing records during log compaction via {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Creating instances:</strong> For reading, use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)}.\n * To create new batches, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} or one of the static factory methods\n * like {@link #withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...)}.\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread-safety:</strong> {@code MemoryRecords} is <strong>not thread-safe</strong> for\n * mutation operations like filtering or in-place batch updates. The underlying ByteBuffer is mutable\n * and modifications via batch methods (e.g., {@link DefaultRecordBatch#setLastOffset(long)}) directly\n * modify the shared buffer. Multiple threads must not concurrently:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Call {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}</li>\n *   <li>Modify batches obtained via {@link #batches()}</li>\n *   <li>Modify or read the underlying buffer while another thread is modifying it</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * Reading batches and iterating records <em>is</em> thread-safe if the underlying buffer is not\n * concurrently modified. Different instances of MemoryRecords can be safely accessed in parallel.\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Batch iteration:</strong> Lazy; batches are deserialized on first iteration</li>\n *   <li><strong>Record iteration:</strong> Depends on compression; uncompressed is O(1) per record,\n *       compressed requires decompression</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> O(n) where n is the number of records; decompresses if necessary</li>\n *   <li><strong>In-place updates:</strong> O(1) for offset/timestamp updates on DefaultRecordBatch,\n *       but triggers CRC recomputation which is O(batch size)</li>\n *   <li><strong>Memory overhead:</strong> Minimal; uses provided ByteBuffer directly without copying</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see Records\n * @see RecordBatch\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the total number of bytes in this message set that form complete, valid batches.\n     * This may be smaller than {@link #sizeInBytes()} if there are partial (incomplete) batches\n     * at the end of the buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance:</strong> O(n) where n is the number of batches. The result is cached\n     * after the first call. Subsequent calls return the cached value without iteration.\n     *\n     * @return The number of valid bytes covering only complete batches\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n     * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n     *         magic byte, null otherwise\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filters records from this MemoryRecords into a destination ByteBuffer based on a filter function.\n     * This method is used during log compaction to remove expired tombstones and aborted transaction markers.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Filter Logic:</strong> For each batch:\n     * <ol>\n     *   <li>{@link RecordFilter#checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} is called to determine if the batch\n     *       should be completely deleted, retained empty, or checked record-by-record</li>\n     *   <li>For retained batches, {@link RecordFilter#shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)} is called\n     *       for each record to determine inclusion</li>\n     *   <li>Filtered batches are written with updated metadata (timestamps, offsets, delete horizon)</li>\n     * </ol>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance characteristics:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>O(n) where n is the total number of records</li>\n     *   <li>Compressed batches are decompressed using the supplied BufferSupplier; buffer reuse\n     *       significantly improves performance on large compressed batches</li>\n     *   <li>Memory: Output buffer may be reallocated if filtered data exceeds destination buffer capacity</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Memory management:</strong> The supplied decompressionBufferSupplier is used for\n     * temporary buffers during decompression. For optimal performance:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Reuse buffers across multiple filter calls using a caching supplier</li>\n     *   <li>For small batches, allocating large buffers (64KB+) may dominate decompression cost</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * @param filter                      The filter determining which records to retain\n     * @param destinationBuffer           The target ByteBuffer for filtered records. May be reallocated\n     *                                    if insufficient space. The buffer's content after filtering\n     *                                    contains the output.\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier Supplier for decompression buffers. For memory efficiency,\n     *                                    provide a reusing supplier via BufferSupplier methods.\n     * @return A {@link FilterResult} containing: the output buffer (possibly different from input),\n     *         record/byte counts for metrics, and the maximum offset/timestamp of retained records\n     * @see RecordFilter\n     * @see FilterResult\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n            long maxOffset = -1;\n            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n            // Convert records with old record versions\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iterator.next();\n                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n\n                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n\n                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n\n                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n                        containsTombstones = true;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n                }\n            }\n            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n        private final long maxOffset;\n        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n                                  final long maxOffset) {\n            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n            if (maxOffset < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n        }\n\n        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n            return outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRead() {\n            return messagesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRead() {\n            return bytesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRetained() {\n            return messagesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRetained() {\n            return bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public long maxOffset() {\n            return maxOffset;\n        }\n\n        public long maxTimestamp() {\n            return maxTimestamp;\n        }\n\n        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               int maxSize) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n                false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                            int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n                                            SimpleRecord... records) {\n        if (records.length == 0)\n            return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n        int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferStream, magic, compression, timestampType,\n                initialOffset, logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false,\n                partitionLeaderEpoch, sizeEstimate)) {\n            for (SimpleRecord record : records)\n                builder.append(record);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n                                        long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        int endTxnMarkerBatchSize = DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + marker.endTxnMarkerValueSize();\n        ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(endTxnMarkerBatchSize);\n        writeEndTransactionalMarker(buffer, initialOffset, timestamp, partitionLeaderEpoch, producerId,\n                producerEpoch, marker);\n        buffer.flip();\n        return MemoryRecords.readableRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,\n                                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                   EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        boolean isTransactional = true;\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, Compression.NONE,\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, initialOffset, timestamp, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, isTransactional, true, partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                buffer.capacity())\n        ) {\n            builder.appendEndTxnMarker(timestamp, marker);\n        }\n    }\n\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01QvoX8MYufNeuD5tAAMxvX7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01JJ9K3WoMnPmyYSVDC4VrbL","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/documentation.md","content":"# Kafka Record Batch Serialization - Javadoc Documentation\n\n**Repository:** github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b (Apache Kafka mirror)\n**Location:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/`\n**Date:** February 2026\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document summarizes comprehensive Javadoc enhancements added to three critical classes handling Kafka's record batch serialization: `RecordBatch`, `DefaultRecordBatch`, and `MemoryRecords`. These classes are fundamental to Kafka's binary protocol, handling the serialization, deserialization, and manipulation of record batches.\n\n## Classes Documented\n\n### 1. RecordBatch (Interface)\n\n**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java`\n\n#### Enhancements Made\n\n##### Class-Level Documentation\n- **Added thread-safety guarantee:** Explicitly documented that RecordBatch implementations are **not thread-safe**. Multiple threads should not access the same batch concurrently, though different batches can be safely accessed in parallel.\n- **Added usage context:** Clarified that batch instances are typically accessed through `MemoryRecords` or `Records` implementations rather than directly instantiated.\n- **Kafka protocol role:** Documented that batches are a fundamental container for records in both legacy (v0/v1) and modern (v2+) formats.\n\n##### Method-Level Documentation Enhanced\n- **`isValid()`** - Clarified return value semantics and CRC algorithm\n- **`ensureValid()`** - Added @throws documentation for `CorruptRecordException`\n- **`maxTimestamp()`** - Distinguished between CREATE_TIME and LOG_APPEND_TIME semantics; noted role of broker vs. producer timestamps\n- **`baseOffset()` / `lastOffset()`** - Added performance notes; emphasized that `lastOffset()` is more efficient across all magic versions\n- **`producerId()` / `producerEpoch()`** - Documented idempotence and transactional semantics\n- **`baseSequence()` / `lastSequence()`** - Explained role in duplicate detection and sequence number preservation during compaction\n- **`streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)`** - **Significantly enhanced documentation** with:\n  - Performance characteristics for uncompressed vs. compressed batches\n  - Resource management requirements (must close iterator)\n  - Buffer reuse recommendations for memory efficiency\n  - Cross-references to `BufferSupplier` factory methods\n- **`isControlBatch()`** - Clarified that control batches contain metadata records\n- **`offsetOfMaxTimestamp()`** - Detailed algorithm (earliest offset on ties), V0 limitation, and performance warning for large batches\n\n#### Key Documentation Principles Applied\n- **Thread-safety as first concern:** Every interface documents that implementations are not thread-safe\n- **Performance implications:** Methods that may require iteration (e.g., `baseOffset()`, `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()`) are clearly marked as potentially expensive\n- **Cross-references:** {@link} tags used throughout to connect related classes and methods\n- **Semantic clarity:** Timestamp type semantics explained to distinguish producer timestamps from broker-assigned timestamps\n\n---\n\n### 2. DefaultRecordBatch (Concrete Implementation)\n\n**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java`\n\n#### Enhancements Made\n\n##### Class-Level Documentation\nCompletely restructured and expanded to provide comprehensive protocol documentation:\n\n- **Binary Format Schema:** Presented the magic v2+ record batch schema in a clear, formatted block, showing all 12 header fields and their sizes\n- **Compression Handling:** Documented how compressed record data is serialized following the record count\n- **CRC Computation:** Explained CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial usage, field coverage (attributes to end, excluding partition leader epoch), and rationale for epoch exclusion\n- **Log Compaction Semantics:** Detailed preservation of base/last offset and sequence numbers for:\n  - Producer state restoration on broker restart\n  - Duplicate detection via sequence verification\n  - Empty batch retention for producer sequence preservation\n- **Timestamp Field Semantics:** Clarified behavior of BaseTimestamp and MaxTimestamp across timestamp types and compaction scenarios\n- **Batch Attributes:** Documented the 16-bit attributes field layout with bit assignments for compression type, timestamp type, transactional flag, control flag, and delete horizon flag\n- **Thread-Safety:** Clearly stated that DefaultRecordBatch is **not thread-safe**; ByteBuffer is mutable and can be modified by multiple threads. Provided mitigation strategies.\n- **Performance Characteristics:** O(1) header access, O(n) record iteration, lazy CRC validation, decompression bottlenecks noted\n- **Cross-references:** {@link} tags to RecordBatch interface, MemoryRecords, and MemoryRecordsBuilder\n\n##### Method-Level Documentation\n\n**Constructor `DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer)`**\n- Documented buffer reference semantics (not copied)\n- Noted minimum size requirement (RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n\n**`ensureValid()`**\n- Added comprehensive @throws documentation for `CorruptRecordException` conditions\n\n**`baseTimestamp()`**\n- Explained reference timestamp semantics for delta calculations\n- Clarified delete horizon, CREATE_TIME, LOG_APPEND_TIME, and empty batch cases\n- Noted that value depends on timestamp type and delete horizon flag\n\n**`writeTo(ByteBuffer)` and `writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream)`**\n- Documented bulk copy semantics\n- Noted performance efficiency\n- Added @throws for BufferOverflowException with space requirement\n\n**`iterator()`**\n- Documented memory vs. efficiency trade-off for compressed batches\n- Cross-referenced `streamingIterator()` for memory-conscious use cases\n- Guaranteed return of empty iterator for zero-record batches\n\n**`skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier)`**\n- Explained when skipping is beneficial (validation, metrics) vs. ineffective (uncompressed)\n- Noted streaming decompression for memory efficiency\n- Required resource management with close()\n\n**`streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)`** - **Significantly Enhanced**\n- Distinguished streaming (on-demand) decompression from full upfront decompression\n- Documented memory savings for large compressed batches\n- Emphasized buffer reuse impact on decompression performance\n- Required resource management with try-with-resources\n- @throws KafkaException for decompression failures\n- Linked to BufferSupplier factory methods\n\n**`setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType, long)`**\n- Documented CRC recomputation logic (skipped if values unchanged)\n- Noted role in log compaction timestamp updates\n\n**`setLastOffset(long)` and `setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int)`**\n- Documented in-place modification semantics\n- Thread-safety implications (buffer is modified directly)\n\n**Static Methods `writeHeader()`, `writeEmptyHeader()`**\n- Documented batch serialization entry points\n- Parameter semantics (deltas, sizes, flags, counts)\n- CRC computation and positioning\n- Use in batch creation workflows\n\n**`sizeInBytes()` overloads**\n- Documented record-level size aggregation\n- Performance characteristics (iterates through records)\n\n**`incrementSequence()` and `decrementSequence()`**\n- Explained wraparound semantics at Integer.MAX_VALUE and 0\n- Used for sequence number arithmetic in idempotent/transactional semantics\n\n#### Key Principles Applied\n- **Protocol Accuracy:** Detailed binary format documentation for developers implementing Kafka clients\n- **Compaction Semantics:** Clear explanation of why batch headers are retained and how they're updated\n- **Performance Warnings:** Every method that requires iteration or CRC computation is marked\n- **Mutation Safety:** Thread-safety concerns highlighted for all mutating methods\n- **Resource Management:** Iterator methods emphasize close() requirements\n\n---\n\n### 3. MemoryRecords (In-Memory Batch Container)\n\n**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java`\n\n#### Enhancements Made\n\n##### Class-Level Documentation\nSignificantly expanded to provide role clarity and usage patterns:\n\n- **Purpose Statement:** Three primary use cases:\n  1. Reading/deserializing batches from ByteBuffer (network/disk I/O)\n  2. In-place modification (timestamps, offsets) without copying\n  3. Filtering records during log compaction\n- **Creation Methods:** Documented entry points:\n  - `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` for reading\n  - `MemoryRecordsBuilder` for creation\n  - Static factory methods for specific scenarios (transactional, idempotent, etc.)\n- **Thread-Safety:** Detailed thread-safety model:\n  - **Not thread-safe** for mutations (filterTo, batch modifications)\n  - Safe for read-only batch/record iteration if buffer is not modified\n  - Different MemoryRecords instances can be accessed in parallel\n  - Specific multi-thread anti-patterns documented (concurrent filterTo, concurrent modifications)\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Batch iteration is lazy (deserialization on iteration start)\n  - Record iteration complexity depends on compression\n  - Filtering is O(n) with decompression if needed\n  - In-place updates are O(1) for values but trigger O(batch size) CRC recomputation\n  - Minimal memory overhead (direct ByteBuffer, no copying)\n- **Cross-references:** Linked to Records, RecordBatch, DefaultRecordBatch, MemoryRecordsBuilder\n\n##### Method-Level Documentation\n\n**`validBytes()`**\n- Clarified distinction from `sizeInBytes()` (partial batches at end)\n- Documented caching behavior (computed once, cached thereafter)\n- O(n) complexity with n = number of batches\n\n**`filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)`** - **Significantly Enhanced**\n- Detailed filter algorithm (batch retention → record retention)\n- Explained three batch retention modes (DELETE, RETAIN_EMPTY, DELETE_EMPTY)\n- Performance analysis: O(n) with n = record count, decompression overhead, buffer reallocation risk\n- Memory management guidance: buffer supplier reuse, small batch overhead considerations\n- Linked to RecordFilter and FilterResult for deeper understanding\n- Output buffer handling (may differ from input if reallocation needed)\n\n**`batches()`**\n- Documented lazy batch deserialization\n- Iteration semantics (can iterate multiple times, fresh views each time)\n- Concurrency warnings (don't use if buffer is being modified)\n\n**`slice(int, int)`**\n- O(1) operation documentation (creates view, no copy)\n- Relative position adjustment explanation\n- Backing buffer semantics (modifications affect both)\n\n**`readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` (Static Factory)**\n- Documented as primary entry point for reading\n- Buffer reference semantics (no copy, lifecycle managed by caller)\n- Use cases (wrapping network/disk buffers)\n\n**`withRecords()` and `with*Records()` Family (Static Factories)**\n- Documented parameters for each variant (magic, compression, timestamps, producer ID, epoch, sequence, transactional)\n- Default value documentation\n- Buffer allocation and overflow handling\n- Memory efficiency notes for large batches\n\n**`withEndTransactionMarker()`, `withLeaderChangeMessage()`, etc.**\n- Control batch specialization documented\n- Single-batch, single-record semantics\n- Use in transaction/leadership management\n\n#### Key Documentation Principles\n- **Multi-scenario design:** Documented all three usage patterns (read, modify, filter)\n- **Buffer lifecycle:** Emphasized that MemoryRecords doesn't own buffers; caller manages lifecycle\n- **Compaction role:** FilterTo is primary method for log compaction integration\n- **Performance transparency:** O(n) operations clearly marked; lazy operations highlighted\n- **Concurrency boundaries:** Clear statement that filter and modifications are NOT thread-safe\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Standards Applied Across All Classes\n\n### 1. Thread-Safety Declarations\nEvery class includes an explicit thread-safety statement in class-level Javadoc:\n- **RecordBatch:** Not thread-safe (interface default)\n- **DefaultRecordBatch:** Not thread-safe (ByteBuffer mutations)\n- **MemoryRecords:** Conditionally thread-safe (read-only is safe, mutations are not)\n\n### 2. Performance Characteristics\nMethods with non-obvious performance implications are documented:\n- O(1) operations: Header field access, sizeInBytes, magic, etc.\n- O(n) operations: Iteration, filtering, CRC validation, record counting\n- Lazy operations: Batch deserialization, streaming decompression\n- Bottleneck warnings: Decompression overhead, buffer allocation\n\n### 3. Resource Management\nCloseable/AutoCloseable iterators explicitly documented:\n- Must close decompression streams\n- Try-with-resources recommended\n- Buffer supplier reuse for efficiency\n\n### 4. Cross-References\nComprehensive {@link} tags connect:\n- RecordBatch interface to implementations (DefaultRecordBatch, AbstractLegacyRecordBatch)\n- MemoryRecords to builder (MemoryRecordsBuilder)\n- Iterator methods to BufferSupplier factory methods\n- Filter logic to RecordFilter and FilterResult classes\n- Compaction semantics to specific methods (setLastOffset, setMaxTimestamp)\n\n### 5. Semantic Clarity\nProtocol semantics explicitly documented:\n- Timestamp type interpretation (producer timestamp vs. broker timestamp)\n- Sequence number preservation during compaction\n- Partition leader epoch assignment and preservation\n- Compression type encoding in attributes\n- CRC algorithm (CRC-32C) and coverage (attributes to end, excluding partition leader epoch)\n\n### 6. Use Case Guidance\nReal-world usage patterns documented:\n- When to use streamingIterator vs. iterator\n- When to use skipKeyValueIterator (validation, metrics)\n- How to efficiently filter large batches (buffer reuse)\n- When to rely on lazy deserialization\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\n| Class | Changes | Key Enhancements |\n|-------|---------|------------------|\n| **RecordBatch** | Class + 8 methods | Thread-safety, performance notes, semantic clarity for timestamps/sequences, streaming iterator details |\n| **DefaultRecordBatch** | Class + 8 methods | Binary format schema, compaction semantics, CRC details, thread-safety, performance warnings |\n| **MemoryRecords** | Class + 4 methods | Three use-case documentation, thread-safety model, filter algorithm details, buffer reuse guidance |\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. `/workspace/kafka--e678b4b/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java`\n2. These classes are fundamental to Kafka's binary protocol, handling the serialization, deserialization, and manipulation of record batches.\n\n## Classes Documented\n\n### 1. RecordBatch (Interface)\n\n**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java`\n\n#### Enhancements Made\n\n##### Class-Level Documentation\n- **Added thread-safety guarantee:** Explicitly documented that RecordBatch implementations are **not thread-safe**. Multiple threads should not access the same batch concurrently, though different batches can be safely accessed in parallel.\n- **Added usage context:** Clarified that batch instances are typically accessed through `MemoryRecords` or `Records` implementations rather than directly instantiated.\n- **Kafka protocol role:** Documented that batches are a fundamental container for records in both legacy (v0/v1) and modern (v2+) formats.\n\n##### Method-Level Documentation Enhanced\n- **`isValid()`** - Clarified return value semantics and CRC algorithm\n- **`ensureValid()`** - Added @throws documentation for `CorruptRecordException`\n- **`maxTimestamp()`** - Distinguished between CREATE_TIME and LOG_APPEND_TIME semantics; noted role of broker vs. producer timestamps\n- **`baseOffset()` / `lastOffset()`** - Added performance notes; emphasized that `lastOffset()` is more efficient across all magic versions\n- **`producerId()` / `producerEpoch()`** - Documented idempotence and transactional semantics\n- **`baseSequence()` / `lastSequence()`** - Explained role in duplicate detection and sequence number preservation during compaction\n- **`streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)`** - **Significantly enhanced documentation** with:\n  - Performance characteristics for uncompressed vs. compressed batches\n  - Resource management requirements (must close iterator)\n  - Buffer reuse recommendations for memory efficiency\n  - Cross-references to `BufferSupplier` factory methods\n- **`isControlBatch()`** - Clarified that control batches contain metadata records\n- **`offsetOfMaxTimestamp()`** - Detailed algorithm (earliest offset on ties), V0 limitation, and performance warning for large batches\n\n#### Key Documentation Principles Applied\n- **Thread-safety as first concern:** Every interface documents that implementations are not thread-safe\n- **Performance implications:** Methods that may require iteration (e.g., `baseOffset()`, `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()`) are clearly marked as potentially expensive\n- **Cross-references:** {@link} tags used throughout to connect related classes and methods\n- **Semantic clarity:** Timestamp type semantics explained to distinguish producer timestamps from broker-assigned timestamps\n\n---\n\n### 2. DefaultRecordBatch (Concrete Implementation)\n\n**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java`\n\n#### Enhancements Made\n\n##### Class-Level Documentation\nCompletely restructured and expanded to provide comprehensive protocol documentation:\n\n- **Binary Format Schema:** Presented the magic v2+ record batch schema in a clear, formatted block, showing all 12 header fields and their sizes\n- **Compression Handling:** Documented how compressed record data is serialized following the record count\n- **CRC Computation:** Explained CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial usage, field coverage (attributes to end, excluding partition leader epoch), and rationale for epoch exclusion\n- **Log Compaction Semantics:** Detailed preservation of base/last offset and sequence numbers for:\n  - Producer state restoration on broker restart\n  - Duplicate detection via sequence verification\n  - Empty batch retention for producer sequence preservation\n- **Timestamp Field Semantics:** Clarified behavior of BaseTimestamp and MaxTimestamp across timestamp types and compaction scenarios\n- **Batch Attributes:** Documented the 16-bit attributes field layout with bit assignments for compression type, timestamp type, transactional flag, control flag, and delete horizon flag\n- **Thread-Safety:** Clearly stated that DefaultRecordBatch is **not thread-safe**; ByteBuffer is mutable and can be modified by multiple threads. Provided mitigation strategies.\n- **Performance Characteristics:** O(1) header access, O(n) record iteration, lazy CRC validation, decompression bottlenecks noted\n- **Cross-references:** {@link} tags to RecordBatch interface, MemoryRecords, and MemoryRecordsBuilder\n\n##### Method-Level Documentation\n\n**Constructor `DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer)`**\n- Documented buffer reference semantics (not copied)\n- Noted minimum size requirement (RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n\n**`ensureValid()`**\n- Added comprehensive @throws documentation for `CorruptRecordException` conditions\n\n**`baseTimestamp()`**\n- Explained reference timestamp semantics for delta calculations\n- Clarified delete horizon, CREATE_TIME, LOG_APPEND_TIME, and empty batch cases\n- Noted that value depends on timestamp type and delete horizon flag\n\n**`writeTo(ByteBuffer)` and `writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream)`**\n- Documented bulk copy semantics\n- Noted performance efficiency\n- Added @throws for BufferOverflowException with space requirement\n\n**`iterator()`**\n- Documented memory vs. efficiency trade-off for compressed batches\n- Cross-referenced `streamingIterator()` for memory-conscious use cases\n- Guaranteed return of empty iterator for zero-record batches\n\n**`skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier)`**\n- Explained when skipping is beneficial (validation, metrics) vs. ineffective (uncompressed)\n- Noted streaming decompression for memory efficiency\n- Required resource management with close()\n\n**`streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)`** - **Significantly Enhanced**\n- Distinguished streaming (on-demand) decompression from full upfront decompression\n- Documented memory savings for large compressed batches\n- Emphasized buffer reuse impact on decompression performance\n- Required resource management with try-with-resources\n- @throws KafkaException for decompression failures\n- Linked to BufferSupplier factory methods\n\n**`setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType, long)`**\n- Documented CRC recomputation logic (skipped if values unchanged)\n- Noted role in log compaction timestamp updates\n\n**`setLastOffset(long)` and `setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int)`**\n- Documented in-place modification semantics\n- Thread-safety implications (buffer is modified directly)\n\n**Static Methods `writeHeader()`, `writeEmptyHeader()`**\n- Documented batch serialization entry points\n- Parameter semantics (deltas, sizes, flags, counts)\n- CRC computation and positioning\n- Use in batch creation workflows\n\n**`sizeInBytes()` overloads**\n- Documented record-level size aggregation\n- Performance characteristics (iterates through records)\n\n**`incrementSequence()` and `decrementSequence()`**\n- Explained wraparound semantics at Integer.MAX_VALUE and 0\n- Used for sequence number arithmetic in idempotent/transactional semantics\n\n#### Key Principles Applied\n- **Protocol Accuracy:** Detailed binary format documentation for developers implementing Kafka clients\n- **Compaction Semantics:** Clear explanation of why batch headers are retained and how they're updated\n- **Performance Warnings:** Every method that requires iteration or CRC computation is marked\n- **Mutation Safety:** Thread-safety concerns highlighted for all mutating methods\n- **Resource Management:** Iterator methods emphasize close() requirements\n\n---\n\n### 3. MemoryRecords (In-Memory Batch Container)\n\n**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java`\n\n#### Enhancements Made\n\n##### Class-Level Documentation\nSignificantly expanded to provide role clarity and usage patterns:\n\n- **Purpose Statement:** Three primary use cases:\n  1. Reading/deserializing batches from ByteBuffer (network/disk I/O)\n  2. In-place modification (timestamps, offsets) without copying\n  3. Filtering records during log compaction\n- **Creation Methods:** Documented entry points:\n  - `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` for reading\n  - `MemoryRecordsBuilder` for creation\n  - Static factory methods for specific scenarios (transactional, idempotent, etc.)\n- **Thread-Safety:** Detailed thread-safety model:\n  - **Not thread-safe** for mutations (filterTo, batch modifications)\n  - Safe for read-only batch/record iteration if buffer is not modified\n  - Different MemoryRecords instances can be accessed in parallel\n  - Specific multi-thread anti-patterns documented (concurrent filterTo, concurrent modifications)\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Batch iteration is lazy (deserialization on iteration start)\n  - Record iteration complexity depends on compression\n  - Filtering is O(n) with decompression if needed\n  - In-place updates are O(1) for values but trigger O(batch size) CRC recomputation\n  - Minimal memory overhead (direct ByteBuffer, no copying)\n- **Cross-references:** Linked to Records, RecordBatch, DefaultRecordBatch, MemoryRecordsBuilder\n\n##### Method-Level Documentation\n\n**`validBytes()`**\n- Clarified distinction from `sizeInBytes()` (partial batches at end)\n- Documented caching behavior (computed once, cached thereafter)\n- O(n) complexity with n = number of batches\n\n**`filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)`** - **Significantly Enhanced**\n- Detailed filter algorithm (batch retention → record retention)\n- Explained three batch retention modes (DELETE, RETAIN_EMPTY, DELETE_EMPTY)\n- Performance analysis: O(n) with n = record count, decompression overhead, buffer reallocation risk\n- Memory management guidance: buffer supplier reuse, small batch overhead considerations\n- Linked to RecordFilter and FilterResult for deeper understanding\n- Output buffer handling (may differ from input if reallocation needed)\n\n**`batches()`**\n- Documented lazy batch deserialization\n- Iteration semantics (can iterate multiple times, fresh views each time)\n- Concurrency warnings (don't use if buffer is being modified)\n\n**`slice(int, int)`**\n- O(1) operation documentation (creates view, no copy)\n- Relative position adjustment explanation\n- Backing buffer semantics (modifications affect both)\n\n**`readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` (Static Factory)**\n- Documented as primary entry point for reading\n- Buffer reference semantics (no copy, lifecycle managed by caller)\n- Use cases (wrapping network/disk buffers)\n\n**`withRecords()` and `with*Records()` Family (Static Factories)**\n- Documented parameters for each variant (magic, compression, timestamps, producer ID, epoch, sequence, transactional)\n- Default value documentation\n- Buffer allocation and overflow handling\n- Memory efficiency notes for large batches\n\n**`withEndTransactionMarker()`, `withLeaderChangeMessage()`, etc.**\n- Control batch specialization documented\n- Single-batch, single-record semantics\n- Use in transaction/leadership management\n\n#### Key Documentation Principles\n- **Multi-scenario design:** Documented all three usage patterns (read, modify, filter)\n- **Buffer lifecycle:** Emphasized that MemoryRecords doesn't own buffers; caller manages lifecycle\n- **Compaction role:** FilterTo is primary method for log compaction integration\n- **Performance transparency:** O(n) operations clearly marked; lazy operations highlighted\n- **Concurrency boundaries:** Clear statement that filter and modifications are NOT thread-safe\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Standards Applied Across All Classes\n\n### 1. Thread-Safety Declarations\nEvery class includes an explicit thread-safety statement in class-level Javadoc:\n- **RecordBatch:** Not thread-safe (interface default)\n- **DefaultRecordBatch:** Not thread-safe (ByteBuffer mutations)\n- **MemoryRecords:** Conditionally thread-safe (read-only is safe, mutations are not)\n\n### 2. Performance Characteristics\nMethods with non-obvious performance implications are documented:\n- O(1) operations: Header field access, sizeInBytes, magic, etc.\n- O(n) operations: Iteration, filtering, CRC validation, record counting\n- Lazy operations: Batch deserialization, streaming decompression\n- Bottleneck warnings: Decompression overhead, buffer allocation\n\n### 3. Resource Management\nCloseable/AutoCloseable iterators explicitly documented:\n- Must close decompression streams\n- Try-with-resources recommended\n- Buffer supplier reuse for efficiency\n\n### 4. Cross-References\nComprehensive {@link} tags connect:\n- RecordBatch interface to implementations (DefaultRecordBatch, AbstractLegacyRecordBatch)\n- MemoryRecords to builder (MemoryRecordsBuilder)\n- Iterator methods to BufferSupplier factory methods\n- Filter logic to RecordFilter and FilterResult classes\n- Compaction semantics to specific methods (setLastOffset, setMaxTimestamp)\n\n### 5. Semantic Clarity\nProtocol semantics explicitly documented:\n- Timestamp type interpretation (producer timestamp vs. broker timestamp)\n- Sequence number preservation during compaction\n- Partition leader epoch assignment and preservation\n- Compression type encoding in attributes\n- CRC algorithm (CRC-32C) and coverage (attributes to end, excluding partition leader epoch)\n\n### 6. Use Case Guidance\nReal-world usage patterns documented:\n- When to use streamingIterator vs. iterator\n- When to use skipKeyValueIterator (validation, metrics)\n- How to efficiently filter large batches (buffer reuse)\n- When to rely on lazy deserialization\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\n| Class | Changes | Key Enhancements |\n|-------|---------|------------------|\n| **RecordBatch** | This class encapsulates the\n    47→ * binary serialization format of a Kafka record batch, including the batch header and all contained\n    48→ * records.\n    49→ *\n    50→ * <p><strong>Binary Format:</strong> The batch follows the RecordBatch schema:\n    51→ * <pre>\n    52→ * RecordBatch =>\n    53→ *  BaseOffset => Int64\n    54→ *  Length => Int32\n    55→ *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n    56→ *  Magic => Int8\n    57→ *  CRC => Uint32\n    58→ *  Attributes => Int16\n    59→ *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32\n    60→ *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n    61→ *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n    62→ *  ProducerId => Int64\n    63→ *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n    64→ *  BaseSequence => Int32\n    65→ *  RecordsCount => Int32\n    66→ *  Records => [Record]\n    67→ * </pre>\n    68→ * When compression is enabled, the compressed record data follows immediately after the record count.\n    69→ *\n    70→ * <p><strong>CRC Computation:</strong> The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used and covers all\n    71→ * bytes from the attributes field through the end of the batch. The partition leader epoch field is\n    72→ * excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation when the broker assigns a leader epoch.\n    73→ *\n    74→ * <p><strong>Log Compaction:</strong> Unlike older message formats, magic v2 batches preserve the\n    75→ * first and last offset/sequence numbers from the original batch during log compaction. This preservation\n    76→ * is critical for: (1) restoring producer state on broker restart, and (2) duplicate detection via\n    77→ * sequence number verification. Empty batches may be retained to preserve producer sequence information\n    78→ * until a new sequence is written or the producer ID expires.\n    79→ *\n    80→ * <p><strong>Timestamp Semantics:</strong>\n    81→ * <ul>\n    82→ *   <li>BaseTimestamp: For CREATE_TIME, reflects the first record's timestamp. After compaction,\n    83→ *       reflects the first remaining record. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, set by the broker. If the\n    84→ *       delete horizon flag is set, BaseTimestamp contains the horizon timestamp for cleanup.</li>\n    85→ *   <li>MaxTimestamp: For CREATE_TIME, the maximum timestamp among current records and updated during\n    86→ *       compaction. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, set by the broker and preserved across compaction.</li>\n    87→ * </ul>\n    88→ *\n    89→ * <p><strong>Batch Attributes:</strong> Encoded in a 16-bit field with the following threads.\n   101→ * To ensure thread-safety: (1) do not share batch instances across threads, or (2) use external\n   102→ * synchronization if sharing is necessary, or (3) wrap the batch operations with immutable views.\n   103→ * Multiple distinct batch instances can be safely accessed in parallel from different threads.\n   104→ *\n   105→ * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n   106→ * <ul>\n   107→ *   <li>Header access (offset, timestamp, producer id, etc.) is O(1)</li>\n   108→ *   <li>Record iteration is O(n) but amortized per-record cost is minimal for uncompressed The underlying ByteBuffer is mutable\n    62→ * and modifications via batch methods (e.g., {@link DefaultRecordBatch#setLastOffset(long)}) directly\n    63→ * modify the shared buffer. Multiple threads must not concurrently:\n    64→ * <ul>\n    65→ *   <li>Call {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}</li>\n    66→ *   <li>Modify batches obtained via {@link #batches()}</li>\n    67→ *   <li>Modify or read the underlying buffer while another thread is modifying it</li>\n    68→ * </ul>\n    69→ *\n    70→ * Reading batches and iterating records <em>is</em> thread-safe if the underlying buffer is not\n    71→ * concurrently modified. This class provides memory-efficient access to serialized Kafka\n * record batches without requiring disk I/O.\n *\n * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong> MemoryRecords is used for three primary scenarios:\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading:</strong> Deserializing record batches from a ByteBuffer (e.g., from fetch responses)</li>\n *   <li><strong>In-place modification:</strong> Updating batch metadata (timestamps, offsets) without copying</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> Removing records during log compaction via {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Creating instances:</strong> For reading, use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)}.\n * To create new batches, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} or one of the static factory methods\n * like {@link #withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...)}.\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread-safety:</strong> {@code MemoryRecords} is <strong>not thread-safe</strong> for\n * mutation operations like filtering or in-place batch updates. The underlying ByteBuffer is mutable\n * and modifications via batch methods (e.g., {@link DefaultRecordBatch#setLastOffset(long)}) directly\n * modify the shared buffer. Multiple threads must not concurrently:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Call {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}</li>\n *   <li>Modify batches obtained via {@link #batches()}</li>\n *   <li>Modify or read the underlying buffer while another thread is modifying it</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * Reading batches and iterating records <em>is</em> thread-safe if the underlying buffer is not\n * concurrently modified. Different instances of MemoryRecords can be safely accessed in parallel.\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Batch iteration:</strong> Lazy; batches are deserialized on first iteration</li>\n *   <li><strong>Record iteration:</strong> Depends on compression; uncompressed is O(1) per record,\n *       compressed requires decompression</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> O(n) where n is the number of records; decompresses if necessary</li>\n *   <li><strong>In-place updates:</strong> O(1) for offset/timestamp updates on DefaultRecordBatch,\n *       but triggers CRC recomputation which is O(batch size)</li>\n *   <li><strong>Memory overhead:</strong> Minimal; uses provided ByteBuffer directly without copying</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see Records\n * @see RecordBatch\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the total number of bytes in this message set that form complete, valid batches.\n     * This may be smaller than {@link #sizeInBytes()} if there are partial (incomplete) batches\n     * at the end of the buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Performance:</strong> O(n) where n is the number of batches. The result is cached\n     * after the first call. Subsequent calls return the cached value without iteration.\n     *\n     * @return The number of valid bytes covering only complete batches\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {","numLines":100,"startLine":44,"totalLines":920}}}
